name: Hisandhercravings
review: Last evening, I took Payal out for a sundowner date, looking for a place with good vibes, great food, and a setting that feels elevated yet relaxed. Soraia delivered on every front. The rooftop ambience at dusk is simply spectacular — the light, the greenery, the quiet luxury of it all. Service was warm without being intrusive, and the tasting menu felt like a genuine culinary journey. Both classic and brilliantly surprising.

name: Urmi Chhapia
review: Soraia is a very pretty restaurant with panoramic views across the city. The food and cocktails are excellent — each plate arrived with a story and each cocktail was a composition in itself. The staff are courteous and attentive without hovering. The Omakase Bar experience is unlike anything else in Mumbai, and I say that as someone who has been to most of the city's fine-dining establishments.

name: Adttya Verma
review: A truly elevated experience. The botanical interiors make you feel transported to another era entirely. Every course was thoughtfully composed and I particularly loved the Indian-European fusion approach — familiar flavours reimagined with genuine restraint and technique. Would strongly recommend the omakase format for a special occasion. The only small note: parking on a weekend evening can be a challenge.

name: Priya Mehta
review: Perfect anniversary dinner. The attention to detail — from the candlelit table setting to the handwritten seasonal menu — was impeccable throughout. Chef's pairings showed genuine mastery and the cocktail programme is inventive without being gimmicky. The team even arranged a small personalised dessert as a surprise. We will absolutely be back, and have already recommended Soraia to everyone we know.

name: Rahul Joshi
review: Worth every rupee. The service is gracious without being stiff and there is a warmth to this place that simply cannot be manufactured. The food has real soul — deeply rooted in Indian flavour memory but completely open to European technique and sensibility. Sitting at the omakase bar solo was one of the more memorable dining experiences I have had in this city. This is what fine dining in Mumbai should feel like.

name: Sanjana Rao
review: We came for a team dinner and Soraia exceeded every expectation. The private dining setup was elegant without feeling corporate, and the chef's custom menu for the evening was a genuine conversation piece. Each course sparked discussion and delight in equal measure. The botanical wine list is thoughtfully curated and the sommelier's pairings were inspired. A restaurant with a distinct point of view — rare and refreshing.

name: ADI
review: lovely food i liked it to much
